meta_title: For Students: Claude Code skills setup to Solve slow development speed (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to Claude Code skills setup and overcome slow development speed. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Claude Code Setup: Build Faster as a Student Developer
content.seo_description: Master Claude Code skills setup to eliminate slow development. Follow this student-focused checklist to ship your first AI project with Antigravity tools.
content.answer_block: Slow development stems from context switching and manual boilerplate. By integrating Claude Code into your terminal and leveraging Antigravity for architectural scaffolding, you automate the repetitive heavy lifting. This setup transforms your CLI into an intelligent pair-programmer, allowing you to focus on high-level logic rather than syntax errors.
content.intro:
You stare at the blinking cursor, the weight of a blank IDE pressing down as the clock ticks toward your deadline. You know the logic, but the friction of scaffolding, debugging, and syntax lookups turns a two-hour task into an all-day ordeal. This isn't a lack of talent; it's a lack of the right leverage.

Welcome to the new era of AI-assisted engineering. By configuring Claude Code as your primary terminal agent and pairing it with Antigravity for rapid architectural structure, you bypass the beginner's bottleneck. We aren't just writing code; we are orchestrating a system that writes with us, letting you build like a senior engineer while you're still mastering the fundamentals.

steps.title: Environment Initialization
steps.description: Configure your terminal workspace to allow Claude Code to access your project files and local environment variables securely.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a standard project directory structure for a full-stack AI application including /src, /tests, and a README.md.

steps.title: Architectural Scaffolding
steps.description: Use Antigravity to define your app's core data models, ensuring Claude Code understands the schema before you start writing business logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a schema definition for a user-profile management system using TypeScript interfaces.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement the TypeScript interfaces defined by Antigravity in /src/types'

steps.title: Contextual Code Generation
steps.description: Feed specific project requirements into Claude Code to generate boilerplate, reducing the time spent on repetitive tasks.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Outline the API route structure for a RESTful service that connects to a vector database.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create Express.js routes based on the provided API outline'

steps.title: Automated Unit Testing
steps.description: Ensure your code is production-ready by using Claude Code to write tests for your newly created functions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate Jest test cases for the authentication controller logic.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Write unit tests for the auth controller and verify they pass'

steps.title: Optimization & Refactoring
steps.description: Review the generated code for performance bottlenecks and clean up unused imports or redundant logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the codebase for potential memory leaks and performance optimizations in the data processing loop.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Refactor the processing loop for better efficiency and clean up the code'

pro_tips: Always provide a 'style guide' file in your root directory so Claude Code learns your specific naming conventions.
Use Antigravity to generate documentation headers automatically so your code is self-documenting from day one.
Keep your Claude Code sessions focused by deleting the .claude_history file when starting a completely different feature module.
Leverage the 'review' command in Claude Code to have it critique its own code before you commit it to your main branch.
Map your most frequent terminal commands to aliases in your .zshrc to trigger Claude Code workflows in milliseconds.
content.next_steps: Once your first feature is live, deploy your project to a cloud provider like Vercel or Railway and document your process in a portfolio blog post.
content.conversion_block: You now have the exact workflow used by top-tier engineers to ship faster and learn deeper. Don't let your ideas stay trapped in your head—execute them with precision and speed. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
